# herdr-v86

> A 32-bit Alpine Linux development guest running in the v86 browser emulator,
> with optional full IPv4 networking and gateway-free browser RPC tools.

Canonical documentation:

- [Guest tools reference](https://avirads.github.io/herdr-v86/docs/guest-tools.md): Syntax, examples, limits, exit behavior, and troubleshooting for vmfetch, vmclip, vmexport, vmgithub, vmai, vmllm, file import, and VM snapshots.
- [Agent instructions](https://avirads.github.io/herdr-v86/AGENTS.md): Operational constraints and rules for coding agents working in the guest.
- [Deep Agents coding agent](https://avirads.github.io/herdr-v86/docs/deep-agent.md): Native planning, filesystem, shell, subagents, skills, approval boundary, and limits.
- [Browser bridge](https://avirads.github.io/herdr-v86/network/docs/host-bridge.md): Architecture, browser security model, and bridge limitations.
- [IPv4 gateway](https://avirads.github.io/herdr-v86/network/README.md): Linux TAP, DHCP, DNS, NAT, secure sessions, and deployment.
- [Project README](https://avirads.github.io/herdr-v86/README.md): Artifacts, hosting requirements, and known issues.

Important constraints:

- v86 is 32-bit x86; x86-64 binaries cannot execute.
- vmfetch uses browser fetch and is subject to CORS and mixed-content policy.
- Full DHCP, DNS, ICMP, TCP, and UDP require the external Linux gateway.
- vmllm uses herdr's built-in page-local WebGPU LiteRT-LM provider; it does not run inference inside the guest and requires no extension.
- Browser-import and export files are limited to 8 MiB; vmfetch responses to 16 MiB.
- Deep Agents maps `/` to `/root/project`; reads are automatic, while mutations and all shell commands require confirmation.
